heading: Exchange of information
label: 1628 Exchange of information
notes: Amendments2020—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c) and struck out former subsec. (c). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary may authorize the Customs Service to exchange information with any government agency of a NAFTA country, as defined in , if the Secretary—Pub. L. 116–113section 3301(4) of this title“(1) reasonably believes the exchange of information is necessary to implement chapter 3, 4, or 5 of the North American Free Trade Agreement, and“(2) obtains assurances from such country that the information will be held in confidence and used only for governmental purposes.”1993—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 103–182

children.text: insure compliance with any law or regulation enforced or administered by the Customs Service;
administer or enforce multilateral or bilateral agreements to which the United States is a party;
assist in investigative, judicial and quasi-judicial proceedings in the United States; and

an action comparable to any of those described in paragraphs (1) through (4) undertaken by a foreign customs or law enforcement agency, or in relation to a proceeding in a foreign country.1So in original. Probably should be “(3)”.1

children.heading: Nondisclosure and uses of information provided
children.label: b Nondisclosure and uses of information provided

children.text: Information may be provided to foreign customs and law enforcement agencies under subsection (a) only if the Secretary obtains assurances from such agencies that such information will be held in confidence and used only for the law enforcement purposes for which such information is provided to such agencies by the Secretary.
No information may be provided under subsection (a) to any foreign customs or law enforcement agency that has violated any assurances described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Government agency of USMCA country
children.label: c Government agency of USMCA country
A
children.text: reasonably believes the exchange of information is necessary to implement chapter 2, 4, 5, 6, or 7 of the USMCA; and

children.label: B
children.text: obtains assurances from such agency that the information will be held in confidence and used only for governmental purposes.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 2 Definitions
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “USMCA” and “USMCA country” have the meanings given those terms in .section 4502 of this title
